name: TEMPI
description: Six-channel polyphonic time-shifting clock. Per-channel multiplier/divider/phase of a Leading Tempo. 64 States in 4 Banks, recallable via CV. Shift, Run/Stop, Mute and Mesh modes.

controls.name: Channel Buttons 1-6
controls.description: Tap to program multiples/divisions (Human programming). Hold combos with PGM_A/B for Machine programming and phase editing. Button LEDs flash to indicate current Clock. Colors encode state: blue=active, red=muted, purple=mod-enabled, pink=muted+mod.

controls.name: PGM_A / PGM_B
controls.description: Program buttons. Hold + tap channel buttons to set Divisor/Multiplier values. Double-press for edit pages (Bank Edit, Program Edit, Clock Edit).

controls.name: State Select (Combo Pot)
controls.description: Selects active State 1-16 within current Bank. Acts as attenuator for State Select CV when patched. When Tap Tempo is disabled becomes Leading Tempo control.

inputs.name: External Leading Tempo
inputs.description: Clock input. TEMPI follows after 2 pulses to measure rate. Primary clock source.

inputs.name: State Select Gate
inputs.description: Increments State on rising edge. When Shift mode on, this input triggers Shift instead.

inputs.name: State Select CV
inputs.description: Absolute State selection. Takes priority over State Select Gate. 0V-5V maps across 16 States of current Bank.

inputs.name: Mod Gate
inputs.description: Triggers Shift and/or Run/Stop for Mod-enabled channels (purple). Behavior set in Program Edit page.

outputs.name: CH 1-6 Outputs
outputs.description: Six independent clock outputs — each a programmable multiple/division/phase-offset of Leading Tempo. Default 50% duty cycle; switchable to 10ms triggers per channel (Clock Edit).

behaviors.name: Human programming
behaviors.description: Tap a channel button at the rate you want that channel to run. TEMPI compares to Leading Tempo and derives multiplier/divisor automatically. Resolution (100/50/25%) set on Program Edit page — 100% for strict integer ratios, 25% for free/loose relationships.

behaviors.name: Machine programming (divisor)
behaviors.trigger: hold PGM_A + tap channel button N times
behaviors.description: Programs channel to ÷N of Leading Tempo. N counts taps (max 32). Hold PGM_A + press PGM_B secondary for double divisor (÷2^y). Multi-channel: tap multiple channel buttons while holding PGM_A.

behaviors.name: Machine programming (multiplier)
behaviors.trigger: hold PGM_B + tap channel button N times
behaviors.description: Programs channel to ×N of Leading Tempo. PGM_B adds MULT mode. Hold PGM_B + press PGM_A secondary for double multiplier. Fine increments/decrements: hold channel + tap PGM_A (slower) or PGM_B (faster).

behaviors.name: Phase programming
behaviors.description: Enters Phase page. Hold PGM_A + tap channel N times for coarse phase decrement (1 cycle of Leading Tempo per N). PGM_B for coarse increment. Holding channel button + PGM_A/B gives fine phase (¼ cycle) adjustments.

behaviors.name: States and Banks
behaviors.trigger: hold PGM_A + PGM_B → State Edit page
behaviors.description: Stores up to 64 timing scenarios in 4 Banks of 16. Recall, store, copy, paste and Mutate operations available. State LED color indicates Bank (Blue/Orange/Pink/White). Nothing is saved across power cycles without explicit Store.

behaviors.name: Shift mode
behaviors.description: On Mod gate rising edge, Clock values are rotated between Mod-enabled channels (purple). Direction set by Button 4 (CW/CCW/Random). Returns to original values on State change. Creates evolving rhythmic variations without reprogramming.

behaviors.name: Run/Stop modes
behaviors.trigger: Mod-enable channels + Mod gate as run/stop
behaviors.description: Run/Stop (red): Mod-enabled channels run on gate high. Run/Stop All (blue, 'James Cigler'): all channels run/stop; enabled channels preserve phase, disabled reset in phase. Alt Run/Stop (purple, 'Robert A.A. Lowe'): inverse logic. Mod input toggled or momentary (Button 6).

behaviors.name: Mute
behaviors.trigger: press PGM_A to enter Mute page
behaviors.description: Tap channel buttons to mute/unmute. Muted = red. Mutes are not Shifted (a trick: muted via large division = shiftable mute). New clocks can be programmed into muted channels — useful for cueing changes in performance.

behaviors.name: Retrigger blocking / integer masking
behaviors.trigger: Mod gate as integer of Leading Tempo → Run/Stop
behaviors.description: When Mod input signal is an integer division of Leading Tempo (e.g. 1/1, 1/2, 1/4), Run/Stop can also act as a channel Reset that preserves phase with Leading Tempo — useful for compositional structure.

behaviors.name: Select Bus follower
behaviors.description: Set TEMPI to Follow Select Bus messages from a René (Make Noise) or Macro Machines Storage Strip. TEMPI receives State changes, Store, Multi-Paste and Mesh operations over the INT.CV bus line. Tempo LED turns green when Following.

behaviors.name: Tap Tempo from Channel 1
behaviors.trigger: Clock Edit → Button 4 enables Leading Tap Tempo
behaviors.description: When enabled, tapping Channel 1 Button sets Leading Tempo. When disabled, State Select Combo Pot/CV becomes Leading Tempo control. Button 2-6 switches per-channel clock width between 50% duty cycle (blue) and 10ms trigger (off).

behaviors.name: Internal delay when clock rate changes
behaviors.description: When Leading Tempo changes rate, TEMPI needs 2 clock pulses to re-measure and lock. For DAW sync, include ~2 bars of silence at the start of a song to allow lock-in. Or use Run/Stop All for DAW-reliable start.

patchIdeas.name: Polyrhythmic percussion loop
patchIdeas.patch: Master clock → Leading Tempo · CH 1 set 1/1 (gate main kick) · CH 2 Machine-programmed to ×3 (hi-hats in triplet) · CH 3 ÷2 (snare on 2nd half), CH 4 ÷3 (perc accent) · CH 5 Fibonacci State (Bank A State 7) · state select via knob auditions presets live · instant polyrhythm bed.
patchIdeas.diagram:
TEMPI:
* CH 1: ×1
* CH 2: ×3
* CH 3: ÷2
* CH 4: ÷3

- Master Clock (Clk) c> TEMPI (Leading Tempo)
- TEMPI (CH 1 Out) c> Kick (Trig)
- TEMPI (CH 2 Out) c> HiHat (Trig)
- TEMPI (CH 3 Out) c> Snare (Trig)
- TEMPI (CH 4 Out) c> Perc (Trig)

patchIdeas.name: CV-scanned State sequencer
patchIdeas.patch: Master clock → Leading Tempo · slow LFO or Pressure Points CV → State Select CV (combo pot ~50%) · TEMPI walks through States in current Bank as CV sweeps · each State is a different rhythmic feel · ideal for song-section morphing without touching the module.
patchIdeas.diagram:
TEMPI:
* States 1-8: programmed feels

- Master Clock (Clk) c> TEMPI (Leading Tempo)
- LFO (Out) >> TEMPI (State Select CV)
- TEMPI (CH 1 Out) c> Destination A (Clk)
- TEMPI (CH 2 Out) c> Destination B (Clk)

patchIdeas.name: Shift variations live
patchIdeas.patch: Program 3 channels for related rhythm · enter Program Edit and enable Shift (Button 4 red) · Mod-enable those 3 channels (purple) · rhythmic gate → Mod Gate · each Mod pulse rotates clock values between the 3 channels producing live rhythmic variations that return on State change. Button 4 purple = Random shift (RODENT mode).
patchIdeas.diagram:
TEMPI:
* CH 2, 3, 5: Mod-enabled (purple)
* Shift: CW (red)

- Master Clock (Clk) c> TEMPI (Leading Tempo)
- Variation Gate (Gate) g> TEMPI (Mod Gate) // each pulse rotates rhythms
- TEMPI (CH 2 Out) c> Voice A (Trig)
- TEMPI (CH 3 Out) c> Voice B (Trig)
- TEMPI (CH 5 Out) c> Voice C (Trig)

patchIdeas.name: Run/Stop All for song intro
patchIdeas.patch: Button 5 blue (Run/Stop All) · Button 6 red (Momentary) · all channels Mod-enabled · Mod Gate held low until intro arrives, then high · every channel starts together in phase (disabled channels) or holding programmed phase (enabled channels) · clean song start without drift.
patchIdeas.diagram:
TEMPI:
* Run/Stop All: enabled
* All CH Mod-enabled

- Master Clock (Clk) c> TEMPI (Leading Tempo)
- Song Gate (Gate) g> TEMPI (Mod Gate) // high = run, low = stop all
- TEMPI (CH 1 Out) c> Voice 1 (Trig)
- TEMPI (CH 2 Out) c> Voice 2 (Trig)

patchIdeas.name: Stutter via Mod-reset
patchIdeas.patch: Program CH 3 to 1/1 · Mod-enable only CH 3 · gate at integer of tempo → Mod Gate · Run/Stop mode momentary · each Mod Gate high resets CH 3's phase — stutter/ratchet effect synced to master · mute CH 3 via Mute page to silence, unmute to perform.
patchIdeas.diagram:
TEMPI:
* CH 3: ×1, Mod-enabled
* Run/Stop: Momentary

- Master Clock (Clk) c> TEMPI (Leading Tempo)
- Performance Gate (Gate) g> TEMPI (Mod Gate)
- TEMPI (CH 3 Out) c> Drum (Trig) // stutters on gate

patchIdeas.name: Drive René via TEMPI
patchIdeas.patch: Program CH 1 = 1/1, CH 2 = ÷4 · CH 1 → René X-CLK · CH 2 → René Y-CLK · Cartesian channel advances 1-D while X is fast · experiment with CH 3/4 → X-Mod / Y-Mod for additional modifier rhythms · compact polyrhythmic Cartesian.
patchIdeas.diagram:
TEMPI:
* CH 1: ×1
* CH 2: ÷4
* CH 3: ÷3

- Master Clock (Clk) c> TEMPI (Leading Tempo)
- TEMPI (CH 1 Out) c> René (X-CLK)
- TEMPI (CH 2 Out) c> René (Y-CLK)
- TEMPI (CH 3 Out) g> René (X-Mod)

patchIdeas.name: Clock Murmur (feedback loop)
patchIdeas.patch: TEMPI CH 1 → René X-CLK (Snake Mode ON) · René X-Gate → TEMPI Leading Tempo · René's gate pattern feeds TEMPI's rate — TEMPI hiccups each time a gate arrives · use TEMPI CH outputs → LPG strikes (Optomix) for rhythmic pulsing that evolves autonomously.
patchIdeas.diagram:
TEMPI:
* CH 1: ×1
* CH 2: ÷2

- TEMPI (CH 1 Out) c> René (X-CLK)
- René (X-Gate) g> TEMPI (Leading Tempo) // feedback
- TEMPI (CH 2 Out) g> Optomix.CH 1 (Strike)

patchIdeas.name: Select Bus macro-control from René
patchIdeas.patch: Set René to LEAD via Global Page → GL.BUS · Set TEMPI to Follow via Bank Edit → Button 3 · State changes on René automatically change TEMPI state (same number) · M-Paste and Mesh operations on René propagate to TEMPI · macro-compose entire rhythmic environment from René.
patchIdeas.diagram:
TEMPI:
* Follow Select Bus: enabled

René:
* GL.BUS: LEAD

- Master Clock (Clk) c> TEMPI (Leading Tempo)
- René (State Change) >> TEMPI (Select Bus) // via INT.CV bus line
